title: Don't Sweat a Government Shutdown. Stocks May Go UP.
text: Apologies to Ben Franklin, but it seems that there is a third certainty in life besides death and taxes: Republicans and Democrats in Washington not getting along. And while you'd think that the looming government shutdown would be a bad thing for stocks, you'd probably be wrong.

title: Military aid to Ukraine gives a shot in the arm to the U.S. economy
text: American military support for Ukraine as it fends off Russia has given a lift to the struggling industrial side of the U.S. economy.

title: RTX Secures $163M Contract to Aid F135 Propulsion Systems
text: RTX is set to procure spare engines, power modules, special test equipment and special tooling to support F135 propulsion systems for the F-35 Lighting II Joint Strike Fighter aircraft.

title: Northrop Grumman Announces Webcast, Conference Call of Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: FALLS CHURCH, Va., Sept. 26, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) announced today that its third quarter 2023 financial results will be posted on its investor relations website on Oct. 26, 2023. Prior to the market opening, and after the filing of the earnings release on Form 8-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission, the company will issue an advisory release notifying the public of the availability of the complete and full text earnings release on the company's website at http://investor.northropgrumman.com.

title: 3 Stocks That Really, Really Need the Defense Bill to Pass
text: Will the government shut down? Despite the hot rhetoric, it doesn't seem possible.

title: NASA to Taxpayers: The Boeing/Lockheed Space Launch System Is "Unaffordable"
text: Cost estimates for NASA's SLS moon rocket have quadrupled over just the past several years. Crunching the numbers, the GAO is all but ready to conclude that NASA cannot afford to keep building SLS rockets.

title: Northrop Grumman Elects Robert Fleming Corporate Vice President and President, Space Systems
text: FALLS CHURCH, Va., Sept. 21, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) announces that its board of directors has elected Robert Fleming corporate vice president and president, Space Systems sector, effective October 9, 2023. Fleming will succeed Tom Wilson, who has chosen to take a new role in the company for personal reasons.

title: RTX Secures a $418M Deal to Support Stinger Missile
text: RTX is set to provide Stinger missile upgrades and replacement.

title: China sanctions Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin over arms sales to Taiwan
text: China has decided to apply sanctions against aerospace and defence firm Northrop Grumman Corp and peer Lockheed Martin Corp for providing weapons to Taiwan, the Chinese foreign ministry said on Friday.

title: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Wins Deal to Aid E-2D Advanced Hawkeye
text: Northrop Grumman (NOC) is set to provide the technological upgrades to E-2D Advanced Hawkeye aircraft.

title: Northrop Grumman Corp (NOC) 2023 Morgan Stanley's 11th Annual Laguna Conference (Transcript)
text: Northrop Grumman Corp. (NYSE:NOC ) Morgan Stanley's 11th Annual Laguna Conference September 13, 2023 11:10 AM ET Company Participants Kathy Warden - Chairman & Chief Executive Officer Dave Keffer - Corporate Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Kristine Liwag - Head of Aerospace and Defense Research here at Morgan Stanley Kristine Liwag Hi, good morning everyone. I'm Kristine Liwag, Head of Aerospace and Defense Research here at Morgan Stanley.

title: Northrop Grumman: Air Force B-21 stealth bomber starts engine tests
text: Northrop Grumman Corp. NOC, -0.22% said late Tuesday that it started engine runs as part of the testing of its B-21 Raider “stealth” bomber. The program's ground testing continues to “progress,” the defense giant said.

title: Northrop Grumman's B-21 Raider Continues Progression to Flight Test
text: The B-21 Raider continues to progress in ground testing with the commencement of engine runs at Northrop Grumman's facilities in Palmdale, Calif. (Photo Credit: Northrop Grumman)

title: RTX Secures $80.3M Contract to Boost AIM-9X Missile Production
text: RTX clinches an $80.3 million contract to enhance capacity for the production of AIM-9X missiles.

title: Viewing From Space - Space Systems Shaping Northrop Grumman's Future
text: Northrop Grumman is a prominent defense company with a rich history in space exploration and a growing market in the era of commercial space exploration. As an ESG investor, the investment recommendations are strictly based on the financial performance and the potential in the space industry. I personally refrain from investing in the company. The company operates in four main sectors, with its Space Systems sector accounting for 31.7% of total revenue in 2022.

title: Northrop (NOC) Wins $46.7 Deal to Support Littoral Combat Ships
text: Northrop (NOC) secures a $46.7 million modification deal to provide engineering and sustainment support for Littoral Combat Ships.

title: RTX Wins a $221M Contract for Supporting F135 Propulsion System
text: RTX is set to provide material and support equipment, program administrative, labor, supplies, services, planning and unit level support equipment for F135 propulsion system.

title: Northrop Grumman: My Favorite High-Tech Investment
text: Northrop Grumman is a highly advanced defense contractor with a focus on next-gen areas like hypersonics and space. NOC has tremendous growth potential in space and related industries, with its Space Systems segment generating over $12 billion in revenues. The company is well-positioned in the fast-growing space industry, with a high-tech portfolio and a strong domestic customer base.

title: Northrop (NOC) Wins Contract to Aid E-2D Advanced Hawkeye Jet
text: Northrop (NOC) is set to offer non-recurring engineering support for the replacement of obsolete parts within the position tracking system for the E-2D Advanced Hawkeye.

title: RTX Secures Contract to Support Maintenance of AESA Radar
text: RTX is set to provide engineering and technical services for the installation, operation, and maintenance of the F/A-18 and EA-18G aircraft's AESA.

title: RTX Secures $192M Air Force Contract to Buy AMRAAM Weapons
text: RTX is set to purchase fielded AMRAAM weapons from various sources.

title: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Secures Contract to Aid MQ-4C Triton
text: Northrop Grumman (NOC) is set to provide for the installation of MQ-4C Triton ground segments mobile operating base (MOB).

title: 3 Defense Stocks to Buy and Hold Until 2030
text: Last year, global military expenditure increased by 3.7% in real terms to touch new highs of $2.24 trillion. It's noteworthy that military spending in Europe witnessed the steepest year-on-year increase in the last 30 years.

title: 7 Defensive Stocks to Buy as the Market Suffers Indigestion
text: Although the market ended last Friday on a positive note, that might not be enough to dismiss the concept of defensive stocks to buy. Yes, CNBC reported that with the Dow Jones closing more than 100 points to kick off September, the index notched its best week since July.

title: Cleaning Up Space Trash: A Billion-Dollar Opportunity?
text: Orbital debris is posing an increasing danger to rockets and satellites in Earth's orbit. The European Space Agency and NASA are researching how this space trash might be cleaned up.

title: SpaceX Dominates Space Launch, But Northrop and Lockheed Still Have a Future in Space
text: With 58 launches year to date, SpaceX is America's biggest rocket launcher by a mile. But when it comes to building satellites for the military, SpaceX rivals Northrop Grumman and Lockheed Martin are at the head of the class.
